_comment: Drill response-beat demo (issue #877, drill mode -- no enemy): two 40-man Infantry blocks side by side, a veteran (left, atomic_response_s 0.05) and a raw levy (right, atomic_response_s 0.6). Both are box-selected (the drag spans ticks 10-26), then given ONE right-wheel command (C) at tick 40. The veteran's block is already swinging about its hinge while the levy still stands square, bracing for over half a second before its own swing begins -- the new per-unit atomic-order response delay, the beat between a drill command and the men actually starting the evolution. A wheel is used (rather than an in-place turn) because its sweeping arc is unmistakable in motion, so the stagger between the two start times reads clearly. Driven live through the real SelectionManager by tools/demo/DemoInputRecorder.gd; world coordinates; physics ticks (60/s).
